2. The following table lists the atomic numbers of some elements in Group I of the periodic table.
Element
Li
Atomic number
3
Na
11
K

Rb
37
(a)
(i)
What is the special name of this group?
(1 mark)
Group I = Alkali Metal
(ii)
State ONE physical property of this group.
Shiny/silvery
(1 mark)
(b)
In what way are the electronic arrangements of Na and Rb
(i)
similar to each other?
(1 mark)
outer most shell election = 1
(ii)
different from each other?
(1 mark)
Different periods = diffrent election shells.
When Na and Rb are dropped to water respectively, an alkali solution is formed.
Metal (Alkali) + Water solution (Alkali)
(c)
(i)
How would you show that an alkali is formed?
(2 marks)
pH paper
(ii)
State the observations between Na and water
(3 marks)
sodium is lighter then water
(iv)
State which element, Na or Rb, will react more vigorously with water?
Explain your answer.
Rb ✓
More electors.
= More unstable.
